title: Repository convention profile extraction
description: Detect .editorconfig, linter/formatter config, directory layout, and package-manager/build-tooling conventions from a repository; normalize into a ConventionProfile knowledge record with provenance (observed vs inferred per §7.4). Enables Petruk to conform to existing conventions during implementation and to use a reference repo as a baseline when scaffolding a new project. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §2.7, §13.5, §27, Milestone 2.
close_reason: internal/convention.Extract() detects .editorconfig, linter/formatter config (eslint/prettier/golangci-lint/gofmt/stylelint/rustfmt), package manager, monorepo-vs-single layout, and directory naming convention, normalizing into a convention-profile KnowledgeRecord with §27.4-conformant observed/inferred provenance. Scoped to detection only; applying a profile (§27.5) and baseline scaffolding (§27.6) deferred to M3. Covered by 4 tests.

title: RTK integration

close_reason: internal/tools/rtk.go: Supervisor.RunViaRTK()/RTKAvailable() run commands through the user's rtk CLI for token-compressed output (§11.4, §2.1). 2 tests, run for real against installed rtk (skips gracefully if rtk absent). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

close_reason: internal/tools/ripgrep.go: Supervisor.Search() runs rg through the supervised execution path (§14.1). 2 tests, run for real against installed rg (skips gracefully if rg absent, e.g. in an environment without it). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

close_reason: internal/evidence/journal.go: append-only JSONL Journal (§7.5, §19.1) using generated protocol.Event, under .punakawan/runs/<runID>/events.jsonl. Append/List. 2 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: Worktree lifecycle
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/gitops/worktree.go: WorktreeManager implements §11.1 steps 1-3+10 (lock, clean-check, worktree+branch creation, removal) gated by approval (§16) via RequestApproval/Approve/Deny using the existing approvals.Store. No dedicated worktree-creation category exists in §16.1's approval list, so it uses ApprovalRecordOperationDestructiveFilesystemAction as the closest fit (documented in code). Non-blocking file-based per-repo lock (§3.1 workspace locking). Reuses gitops.Inspector.Status for the clean-repo check rather than duplicating git-status parsing. 5 tests: unapproved-create-fails, full request->approve->create->remove happy path, dirty-repo refusal, denied-request refusal, concurrent-lock refusal. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

title: Git repository inspection
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Implemented internal/gitops/inspect.go: Inspector wraps git status/log/diff/rev-parse through tools.Supervisor (read-only, no writes). All operations go through Supervisor.Run for dir-allowlist/timeout/output-cap enforcement. Added inspect_test.go with real git repo fixtures (2 commits + uncommitted change). go build/vet/gofmt/test all clean.

title: Staleness detection
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/knowledge/staleness.go: ContentHash() produces schema-compliant sha256 digests; Store.CheckStale compares a record's stored source.content_hash against a caller-supplied current hash and transitions validity.state to stale on mismatch via Put. No stored hash means no baseline, so it declines to guess. Covered by 5 tests.

title: Knowledge import and export
description: Deliverable of M2: Knowledge and task foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Store.Export/Import in internal/knowledge/importexport.go: JSONL export ordered by id for reproducibility, fail-fast import that routes every record through Put so §7.3/§7.4 provenance validation applies uniformly. Covered by TestExportImportRoundTrip and two rejection tests.

title: Tool supervisor
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: internal/tools/supervisor.go: Supervisor.Run() enforces §11.4 - explicit argv only (no shell), working-dir allowlist, env allowlist (default PATH/HOME/LANG/TMPDIR), per-call timeout via context, output cap via limitedBuffer (truncates, doesn't error), process-group termination on cancel (Setpgid + SIGTERM to -pid, 5s WaitDelay before SIGKILL). POSIX-only, documented; multi-platform deferred to M9. 6 tests including a real timeout test (bug found+fixed: context-cancellation check must run before exec.ExitError check, since a SIGTERM-killed process also surfaces as ExitError). Verified: go test, lint, build pass.

title: Policy engine
description: Deliverable of M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

Default() is conservative (push+default-branch-write denied, external writes require approval). AllowsFilesystemWrite() enforces deny-globs (doublestar, supports **) over write-globs. 3 tests. Verified: go test, lint, build all pass.

WireDependency translates the schema's blocks/discovered-from/requires vocabulary onto bd dep add's types. Covered by 6 tests including a full end-to-end round trip.

Verified: go test with a real dolt sql-server, lint/build pass. Also added dolt install step to CI.

title: Adapter manifest format
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Created docs/adapters/manifest-format.md documenting every adapter manifest field (id, name, version, protocol, runtime, provides, permissions.network/filesystem/secrets, operations.<name>.side_effect/approval) and how the Go core supervisor uses the manifest across the §5.2 lifecycle (start -> initialize -> validate manifest -> grant capabilities -> execute -> receive result -> record evidence -> stop/reuse), referencing protocol/adapter.schema.json as canonical. Created examples/atlassian-playwright/adapter-manifest.example.yaml, the verbatim §5.4 Atlassian Cloud manifest. Validated with a throwaway node script (js-yaml + ajv/dist/2020 + ajv-formats installed via npm install --no-save in scratch dir): YAML parses cleanly and passes ajv.compile(schema)/validate(doc) against protocol/adapter.schema.json with zero errors. No protocol/*.schema.json files modified.

title: JSON-RPC stdio prototype
description: Deliverable of M0: Architecture and protocol foundation. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
close_reason: Newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. TS side: packages/adapter-sdk/src/stdio.ts (serveStdio helper) + prototypeAdapter.ts (initialize validates against generated AdapterManifestSchema, execute/sleep supports cancellation, shutdown exits). Go side: internal/adapters/stdio.go (Client: Start/Call/Shutdown/Kill, ctx-based cancellation sends a cancel notification). Integration test test/integration/adapter_stdio_test.go (build-tag integration): TestInitializeHandshake proves Go can start+handshake a TS adapter with schema validation; TestExecuteCancellation proves a 150ms ctx timeout aborts a 5s sleep op promptly. Verified: make lint/test/integration-test all pass. Satisfies M0 acceptance criteria on adapter start + cancellation/timeout.

Makefile generate/protocol-check targets wired (protocol-check re-generates and git-diffs to catch staleness). Verified: go build/vet, tsc --noEmit, make generate/build/lint/test all pass. acceptance_criteria: A requirement can link to files and tests across repositories; every relation retains source provenance; Beads tasks map back to requirements and Jira keys; knowledge changes can be diffed and versioned; a repository's editorconfig, formatter/linter config, and directory conventions are captured as a ConventionProfile with provenance.
close_reason: M2 complete: all 9 deliverables done (Dolt schema, knowledge service, provenance model, cross-repository relations, Beads integration, requirement-to-task mapping, import/export, staleness detection, convention profile extraction). Full make lint && make test green.

title: M1: Core runtime and local repository workflow
description: CLI, workspace discovery, policy engine, approval storage, tool supervisor, Git worktree lifecycle, JSONL event journal. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Punakawan opens a workspace with multiple repositories; it can inspect repositories without writing; it can create an isolated worktree after approval; every command is auditable and cancellable; default-branch writes are blocked.
close_reason: All 11 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified via CLI: workspace opens with multiple repositories (workspace show), repositories inspected without writing (git status/log/diff/branch, read-only through Supervisor), isolated worktree created after approval (worktree request/approve/create, refuses without approval), every command auditable/cancellable (Supervisor timeout+process-group kill, tested; JSONL event journal available), default-branch writes blocked (policy Default() denies git.default_branch_write and git.push requires approval).

title: M0: Architecture and protocol foundation
description: Repository scaffolding, Go/pnpm workspaces, canonical JSON Schema protocol, JSON-RPC stdio prototype. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Go can start a TypeScript adapter; both sides validate the same message schemas; cancellation and timeout work; CI detects stale generated code.
close_reason: All 9 deliverables closed. Acceptance criteria verified: Go starts a TS adapter (stdio prototype), both sides validate the same schemas (generated Go structs + Zod validators, manifest validated in TestInitializeHandshake), cancellation/timeout work (TestExecuteCancellation), CI detects stale generated code (protocol-check in ci.yml).

title: Add install script for full local Punakawan setup
description: User wants a single script that installs all prerequisites globally (bd, dolt, node/pnpm, go, rtk), builds Punakawan once, collects Atlassian credentials (email + API token or service-account key + site host, e.g. yourteam.atlassian.net) into a global (not per-project) location, writes the global workspace.GlobalConfig adapters entry, and registers punakawan as a user-scope Claude Code MCP server so it attaches to any git-tracked project with zero required per-project scaffolding. The adapter itself derives the Atlassian cloud ID from the site host at runtime (via the unauthenticated tenant_info endpoint) so the user never has to look up or paste a cloud ID. A project workspace.yaml remains fully optional, only for overriding defaults. Must not hardcode secrets into any git-tracked file.
acceptance_criteria: Running the script on a clean macOS machine (bd/dolt/rtk not yet installed) ends with punakawan registered at Claude Code user scope (available in any project directory, no .mcp.json or per-project workspace.yaml required), a global adapters config with the atlassian adapter set up, and credentials stored only in a global file outside any git-tracked directory, not in any workspace.yaml.

title: adapter-atlassian: support Basic auth (email+token) for personal API tokens
description: Atlassian's Rovo MCP server headless auth has two distinct variants confirmed via Context7 + WebFetch of https://support.atlassian.com/atlassian-rovo-mcp-server/docs/configuring-authentication-via-api-token/: personal API tokens (individual user, tied to an email) require 'Authorization: Basic base64(email:api_token)'; service-account API keys use 'Authorization: Bearer <api_key>'. The current mcpClient.ts always sends Bearer, which is wrong for the personal-token+email case a real individual user actually has on hand. Also requires an org admin to enable API token auth for Rovo MCP under Atlassian Administration -> Rovo -> Rovo MCP server -> Authentication before either headless variant works at all.
design: Add optional ATLASSIAN_EMAIL to AtlassianConfig/loadConfigFromEnv (not required, unlike token/cloudId). defaultTransportFactory (or a new buildAuthHeader helper) branches: email present -> Basic base64(email:token); else -> Bearer token, preserving today's default for anyone already relying on Bearer.
acceptance_criteria: mcpClient.ts builds Basic base64(email:token) when ATLASSIAN_EMAIL is set, falls back to existing Bearer behavior when it is not (service-account key case). Tests cover both header-construction paths. All existing adapter-atlassian tests still pass.
close_reason: mcpClient.ts's buildAuthorizationHeader branches on whether ATLASSIAN_EMAIL is set: personal API token (email present) -> Basic base64(email:token); service-account key (no email) -> Bearer token - both configurable, matching the user's explicit ask to support either credential type. Confirmed via two independent WebFetch reads of the official Atlassian docs (configuring-authentication-via-api-token) that these header formats are not interchangeable. Verified: 4 new tests in mcpClient.test.ts (email presence/absence, both header formats incl. base64 round-trip); all 38 adapter-atlassian tests pass; typecheck/build clean.

title: MCP tool: update_jira_task_progress (estimate, worklog, comment on change)
description: When Petruk finishes a task (M6's finish_task_execution), Jira should reflect it: 'when do some changes, punakawan can update the jira, through comment, update the subtask and fill the work load.' New MCP tool update_jira_task_progress(issue_key, story_points?, original_estimate_hours?, worklog_hours?, comment?) that: fills original estimate from jiraworkflow.EstimateHours(story_points) unless original_estimate_hours is explicitly given (Petruk's own estimate overrides the points-derived default, per the user's decision), adds a worklog entry via atlassian.addWorklog when worklog_hours is given, and posts comment via atlassian.addJiraComment when given. All three actions are independently optional - a caller can update just one.
design: Built on punokawan-4ij.1 (invocation), punokawan-4ij.3 (editJiraIssueFields/addWorklog), punokawan-4ij.5 (EstimateHours). Building myself: the other end of the M6 execution loop's Jira integration, ties directly into finish_task_execution.
close_reason: update_jira_task_progress derives original estimate hours via jiraworkflow.Config.EstimateHours(story_points) unless original_estimate_hours is given explicitly (explicit wins, per user decision), writes it as Jira's native timetracking.originalEstimate duration string (fractional hours split into Nh Mm rather than an undocumented decimal), adds a worklog entry, and posts a comment - each of the three independently optional and independently approval-gated. Verified: 6 new unit tests against a fake adapter caller (points-derived estimate, explicit-overrides-points, no-estimate-when-ratio-unconfigured, worklog+comment together, missing-approval error, duration formatting incl. whole/fractional/zero hours); go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... all pass.

title: MCP tool: sync_jira_subtasks and skip-status task graph filtering
description: Two tied-together integration points requested by the user: (1) 'punakawan can skip a jira that sent back to product review' - when building a task graph (internal/tasks.GenerateGraph, M6) from a Jira-sourced requirement, check the requirement's current Jira status against jiraworkflow.ShouldSkip before creating a task for it. (2) 'punakawan can also auto generate subtasks (avoid redundant task)' - a new sync_jira_subtasks MCP tool wrapping atlassian.createJiraSubtask so Petruk's task decomposition can mirror onto real Jira subtasks, not just Beads issues.
design: New MCP tool sync_jira_subtasks(parent_issue_key, subtasks[]) calling call_adapter_operation with atlassian.createJiraSubtask. Skip-check: a small addition to the submit_task_graph handler (or a separate check_jira_skippable tool the caller invokes before submitting) - decide which during implementation based on which reads more naturally against §10's existing flow.
notes: Depends on punokawan-4ij.1, punokawan-4ij.4, punokawan-4ij.5. Building myself: touches the already-built M6 task graph generator.
close_reason: check_jira_skippable reads a requirement's raw Jira status (stored on its KnowledgeRecord by adapter-atlassian's normalizeJiraIssue) against jiraworkflow.Config.ShouldSkip - implemented as a separate pre-check tool rather than modifying internal/tasks.GenerateGraph directly, since silently dropping items there would complicate its already-shipped, tested dependency-wiring semantics for every caller. sync_jira_subtasks wraps atlassian.createJiraSubtask (dedup logic already lives in .4's work) with approval gating. Verified: 4 new tests (2 real MCP wire-protocol tests seeding KnowledgeRecords with jira-sourced status + jira-workflow.yaml config; 2 unit tests against a fake adapter caller for the create/skip/no-approval paths); go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... all pass.

title: MCP tool: request_jira_clarification (comment + status transition)
description: When Semar/Gareng/Petruk need clarification (per §9.2's clarification gate), render the question via the already-built packages/clarification-comments, post it via atlassian.addJiraComment, then look up the configured clarification_status through atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue and call atlassian.transitionJiraIssue with the matching transition id - failing clearly (not silently) if no transition to that status exists from the issue's current state. This is the direct implementation of the user's ask: 'when punakawan challenge a jira and ask for clarification, it can comment the jira and update jira status to sent back to product review.'
design: New MCP tool in internal/mcpserver, built on punokawan-4ij.1's call_adapter_operation plumbing (or a dedicated typed wrapper over Registry+Gate) plus internal/jiraworkflow's clarification_status config. Building myself: ties together adapter invocation, approval gating, and workflow config - the integration point, not an isolated piece.
notes: Depends on punokawan-4ij.1 (adapter invocation), punokawan-4ij.2 (transition ops), punokawan-4ij.5 (config).
close_reason: request_jira_clarification posts a comment via atlassian.addJiraComment, then (only when jira-workflow.yaml configures clarification_status) looks up real workflow transitions via atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue and applies the matching one via atlassian.transitionJiraIssue, erroring clearly if none matches. Comment body is pre-rendered upstream (via packages/clarification-comments' new CLI entry point, added this task) rather than reimplemented in Go, avoiding duplicated templating logic. Verified: 4 new unit tests (tools_jiraclarify_test.go) against a fake adapter caller covering post+transition happy path, unconfigured-status skip, no-matching-transition error, and missing-approval error; go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... all pass; clarification-comments CLI smoke-tested directly and its own 10/10 tests still pass.

title: internal/jiraworkflow: org-specific status/estimation config
description: New workspace-scoped config (.punakawan/jira-workflow.yaml, since status names and estimation scales are per-org/per-project, not a general capability policy): skip_statuses []string, clarification_status string, estimation.scale []float64 (e.g. Fibonacci [1,2,3,5,8,13,21]), estimation.points_to_hours float64. Jira has no universal 'this status means product review' or 'this is the story-point scale' discovery mechanism - status names and board estimation settings are org-configured, per the dashboard-canvas skill's own reliance on statusCategory (generic) vs raw status/labels (org-specific) split. This package makes that split explicit and honest rather than guessing.
design: internal/jiraworkflow/config.go: Load(path) (*Config, error) with a safe empty default when the file does not exist (mirrors policy.Load's pattern). ShouldSkip(statusName string) bool, EstimateHours(storyPoints float64) float64, ValidateStoryPoints(points float64) error (off-scale is a validation error, not silently accepted, since the user explicitly wants estimation-mode awareness, not passthrough).
notes: Delegated: small, self-contained, mirrors internal/policy's existing Load/Default pattern closely.
close_reason: internal/jiraworkflow.Config (Load/Default/ShouldSkip/EstimateHours/ValidateStoryPoints) implements the org-specific status/estimation split grounded in the epic's own research (statusCategory is generic, raw status names/estimation scales are not). Reviewed the subagent's implementation directly: correct empty-scale-means-no-validation and zero-ratio-means-unconfigured semantics, case-insensitive ShouldSkip. Verified: 10/10 tests in jiraworkflow_test.go pass against test/fixtures/jira-workflow.yaml.

title: Atlassian adapter: deduplicated subtask creation
description: Add atlassian.createJiraSubtask: before creating, searches existing children via searchJiraIssuesUsingJql (jql: parent = <parentKey>), compares the candidate's normalized summary against existing subtask summaries (case-insensitive, whitespace-collapsed exact match - not fuzzy, to avoid false-positive skips), and only calls createJiraIssue(cloudId, projectKey, issueTypeName, summary, description, parent, additional_fields) for genuinely new ones. Returns which were created vs skipped as duplicates. issueTypeName for subtasks is discovered via the already-existing getJiraProjectIssueTypesMetadata operation, not hardcoded as 'Sub-task' (naming varies by project).
design: packages/adapter-atlassian/src/operations.ts: createJiraSubtask(client, {parentKey, projectKey, candidates: {summary, description, additionalFields}[]}) -> {created: NormalizedJiraIssue[], skipped: {summary, existingKey}[]}. New manifest op atlassian.createJiraSubtask, side_effect: true, approval: required.
notes: Delegated: builds on the already-confirmed searchJiraIssuesUsingJql/createJiraIssue tools from M4, same fake-server test pattern.
close_reason: atlassian.createJiraSubtask added with exact-match (trimmed/whitespace-collapsed/case-insensitive) dedup against existing children via searchJira JQL, only creating genuinely-new candidates. Deviation from the task description, flagged and accepted: issueTypeName is caller-supplied rather than internally discovered via getJiraProjectIssueTypesMetadata, since that tool isn't implemented anywhere in this codebase (confirmed by the subagent reading the full existing operations.ts first) - noted for whoever picks up .7/.8. Verified independently: pnpm --filter @punakawan/adapter-atlassian typecheck/build/test all clean, 34/34 tests pass.

title: Atlassian adapter: field editing, worklog, and field-metadata discovery
description: Add atlassian.editJiraIssueFields (wraps editJiraIssue(cloudId, issueIdOrKey, fields), write:jira-work) for updating arbitrary fields (story points custom field, native timetracking.originalEstimate), atlassian.addWorklog (wraps addWorklogToJiraIssue(cloudId, issueIdOrKey, timeSpentSeconds, comment?), write:jira-work), and atlassian.getIssueTypeFieldMeta (wraps getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields(cloudId, projectIdOrKey, issueTypeId), read_jira) so the story-points custom field id is discovered per-project rather than hardcoded, since it is never the same customfield_NNNNN id across Jira sites.
design: Same file/manifest pattern as the transition task. editJiraIssueFields and addWorklog declared side_effect: true, approval: required; getIssueTypeFieldMeta side_effect: false. Extend the fake server with matching fixture tools.
notes: Delegated: same established pattern. Coordinate with the transition task (punokawan-4ij.2) on shared fakeAtlassianServer.ts edits - assign sequentially or merge carefully to avoid clobbering.
close_reason: atlassian.editJiraIssueFields, atlassian.addWorklog, atlassian.getIssueTypeFieldMeta added per spec (arbitrary fields passthrough, no hardcoded story-point field id). Reviewed diff directly, approval gating correct on both write ops. Verified independently: pnpm --filter @punakawan/adapter-atlassian typecheck/build/test all clean, 34/34 tests pass.

title: Atlassian adapter: status transition operations
description: Add atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue (wraps the real getTransitionsForJiraIssue(cloudId, issueIdOrKey) tool, read_jira group, confirmed via support.atlassian.com/atlassian-rovo-mcp-server/docs/supported-tools/) returning {id, name, toStatus}[], and atlassian.transitionJiraIssue (wraps transitionJiraIssue(cloudId, issueIdOrKey, transitionId), write:jira-work scope) which performs the transition. Jira workflows require the discovery call first since transition ids are workflow-specific, not status names - do not skip it.
design: packages/adapter-atlassian/src/operations.ts: getTransitionsForJiraIssue and transitionJiraIssue functions mirroring existing getJiraIssue/addJiraComment patterns. manifest.ts: atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue (side_effect: false), atlassian.transitionJiraIssue (side_effect: true, approval: required). Extend test/fakeAtlassianServer.ts with both tools and fixture transition data.
notes: Delegated: mirrors already-established adapter-atlassian patterns exactly, no new architecture.
close_reason: atlassian.getTransitionsForJiraIssue and atlassian.transitionJiraIssue added to operations.ts/manifest.ts/adapter.ts, transition-required-before-status-name design as specified. Reviewed diff directly (manifest declares transitionJiraIssue approval:required, getTransitionsForJiraIssue side_effect:false; adapter.ts dispatch matches manifest op names exactly). Verified independently (not just per subagent report): pnpm --filter @punakawan/adapter-atlassian typecheck/build/test all clean, 34/34 tests pass.

title: Adapter process lifecycle and generic invocation MCP tool
description: Foundational gap: internal/adapters.Start/Client/Gate exist (built in M4) but nothing spawns an adapter subprocess or exposes it to a calling role. New internal/adapters.Registry maps an adapter id (e.g. 'atlassian') to its spawn command + required env vars, lazily starts and reuses one *adapters.Client per adapter id (memoized on app.App, mirroring OpenKnowledge's lazy-singleton pattern), wraps each in a *adapters.Gate using the adapter's own manifest. New MCP tool call_adapter_operation(adapter_id, op, params, run_id, requested_by) routes through Gate.Call, so approval-gated operations (like atlassian.addJiraComment) are enforced exactly as M4 designed, now actually reachable.
design: internal/adapters/registry.go: Registry{specs map[string]AdapterSpec}, AdapterSpec{Command string, Args []string, EnvPassthrough []string}, (*Registry) Client(id string) (*Client, error) lazily calling Start. app.App gets an AdapterRegistry field, wired in Load(). Building myself: process spawning + secret env var passthrough (ATLASSIAN_MCP_TOKEN) is security-sensitive.
notes: Discovered this gap while scoping the Jira epic: grep -rn adapters. internal/mcpserver returned zero hits before this task.
close_reason: Registry.Gate lazily starts an adapter process, discovers its manifest via the new §5.3 capabilities RPC (added to prototypeAdapter.ts/docling-adapter/adapter-atlassian), completes initialize, and memoizes the Gate; Registry.Close shuts down spawned processes. Wired into app.App.Load/Close. New call_adapter_operation MCP tool routes through Gate.Call, enforcing manifest-declared approval gating. Verified: internal/adapters (9 tests, incl. 4 new registry_test.go cases: happy path, memoization, unknown id, Close-shuts-down-process) + 2 new MCP wire-protocol tests in tools_adapters_test.go (end-to-end call against the real spawned prototype adapter, unknown-adapter-id error) all pass; full go build/vet/gofmt clean; go test ./... = 188 passed/28 packages.

title: Jira as source of truth: adapter invocation, workflow-aware sync, subtasks, estimates, worklog
description: Deepens §13.2's Jira/Confluence integration per direct user request: Punakawan must understand org-specific Jira statuses/estimation modes, skip issues already sent back to product review, comment+transition Jira when Semar/Gareng/Petruk need clarification, auto-generate non-redundant subtasks, and auto-fill original estimate/worklog as work progresses. Grounded via Context7 + direct README/skills fetch from github.com/atlassian/atlassian-mcp-server and support.atlassian.com/atlassian-rovo-mcp-server/docs/supported-tools/: confirmed real tools transitionJiraIssue, getTransitionsForJiraIssue, editJiraIssue, addWorklogToJiraIssue, getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields, createJiraIssue (parent+additional_fields for subtasks), searchJiraIssuesUsingJql (for dedup). Discovered mid-scoping: M4's adapter+Gate infrastructure was never actually wired into a live MCP tool - internal/mcpserver has zero references to internal/adapters, so no calling role can invoke any adapter operation yet. That wiring is now this epic's foundational first piece. Scoped as offline/mock-verified per user decision (no live Jira credentials in this environment); estimate auto-fill uses points-to-hours conversion as default, overridable by Petruk's own stated estimate, per user decision.
acceptance_criteria: Punakawan can invoke a real (fixture-tested) Jira adapter operation through the live MCP server. A task-graph generation run against a Jira-sourced requirement skips issues in a configured skip-status list. Requesting clarification posts a real comment and transitions the issue via a real workflow-transition lookup. Generating subtasks does not create duplicates of already-existing subtasks. Finishing a task updates original estimate (points-derived, Petruk-overridable) and adds a worklog entry.
close_reason: All 8 children closed. Acceptance criteria met (offline/mock-verified scope, per user decision): (1) call_adapter_operation reaches a real spawned adapter process over the live MCP server, verified end-to-end; (2) check_jira_skippable correctly excludes Jira-sourced requirements whose stored status matches a configured skip_statuses entry; (3) request_jira_clarification posts a comment then transitions via a real getTransitionsForJiraIssue lookup, failing clearly if no matching transition exists; (4) createJiraSubtask/sync_jira_subtasks dedupe by normalized summary against existing children before creating; (5) update_jira_task_progress fills original estimate from story points via the configured ratio unless an explicit estimate is given (which wins), and independently adds worklog/comment. Also fixed the epic's own foundational gap: M4's adapter/Gate infrastructure is now actually reachable from the running MCP server via the new §5.3 capabilities message and Registry. All work committed in 10 scoped commits; full go build/vet/gofmt/test and pnpm -r test clean throughout.

title: Add CLI/MCP surface for granting or denying pending approvals
description: internal/approvals.Store and gitops.WorktreeManager.Approve/Deny plus internal/adapters.Gate.Approve/Deny exist as Go methods, but nothing lets a human actually call them: punakawan mcp serve exposes no approve_request/deny_request tool, and there is no punakawan approvals CLI subcommand either. Discovered while wiring M6's start_task_execution tool (punakawan-bap.4): it requires an already-approved worktree-creation request, and today the only way to approve one is to call WorktreeManager.Approve directly from Go test code. This blocks real (non-test) usage of every approval-gated M4/M6 tool. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §16 (Approval Model) does not specify how a human actually grants approval, leaving this as an implementation gap rather than a documented design choice.
close_reason: Added `punakawan approvals list/approve/deny` CLI (cmd/punakawan/approvals_cmd.go), backed by a new generic internal/approvals.Store.Resolve method that resolves by id/status/approver alone, working for both worktree-creation and adapter-operation approval records (unlike the existing worktree-only approve/deny commands). Deliberately CLI-only, not an MCP tool: per §16.2, approved_by is the human, distinct from requested_by (the AI role) - exposing approve/deny over MCP would let the same connected client grant its own pending request. Verified: 3 new approvals package tests (approve, unknown-id error, already-resolved error) + 1 new CLI test exercising list/approve/deny against a real adapter-operation-shaped approval id end-to-end; full go build/vet/gofmt/test clean (all 24 packages).

title: MCP server for role invocation
description: punakawan mcp serve (Go, official modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk, stdio transport): exposes semar/gareng/petruk/bagong prompts + build_context_dossier/submit_*_review/submit_*_plan/get_workflow_state/advance_workflow tools. Connected MCP client (e.g. Claude Code) performs the actual reasoning; Punakawan validates+persists results. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §28, ADR-0016.
close_reason: internal/mcpserver serves the 4 role prompts (embedded from prompts/<role>/prompt.md) and 8 tools (build_context_dossier, submit_gareng_review, submit_petruk_plan, submit_semar_synthesis [routes to synthesis or final_plan], submit_bagong_review, get_workflow_state, advance_workflow, plus create_workflow_run as a necessary addition beyond §28.4's literal list since nothing else starts a run) over the go-sdk's stdio transport, wired via 'punakawan mcp serve'. app.App gained a lazy OpenKnowledge()/Close() so the Dolt server only starts for commands that need it. Verified via 6 in-memory-transport tests plus a real stdio JSON-RPC handshake (initialize/tools list/prompts list) against the built binary.

close_reason: internal/workflow persists WorkflowRun state via append-only JSONL, mirroring internal/approvals' Store/Current pattern (§18.2's 'load last durable checkpoint'). New() creates the initial 'created' checkpoint; Advance() appends a checkpoint and updates state, rejecting unknown states and any move out of a terminal state (completed/failed/cancelled) but not enforcing an unstated transition graph, since §18.1 only enumerates states, not a specific edge set. Covered by 7 tests.

Covered by 5 tests using a fake caller (no subprocess needed).

OpenQuestion type is derived from the generated KnowledgeRecord type via indexed access rather than hand-retyped. Established the repo's first TS test setup (node:test). Covered by 10 tests.

title: Discovered-task handling
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: Extend internal/tasks: ReportDiscoveredWork(ctx, sup, dir, store, sourceReq, discoveredFromTaskID, in) calls the existing CreateTaskForRequirement with in.DiscoveredFrom set to discoveredFromTaskID, and applies bd labels ('discovered', 'needs-semar-review') so Semar can find it without a new review-queue subsystem. MCP tool report_discovered_task.
notes: Per §10.4: 'Petruk must not silently increase scope. Newly discovered work becomes a discovered-from task and is reviewed by Semar.' Delegated: thin extension of the already-built internal/tasks primitives.
close_reason: internal/tasks.ReportDiscoveredWork extends the existing createTaskForRequirement helper (refactored to share logic, zero duplication) to force discovered_from and add 'discovered'+'needs-semar-review' bd labels per §10.4's discovery rule, letting Semar find discovered work via bd list --label without a new review-queue subsystem. Exposed as MCP tool report_discovered_task. 8/8 tests pass, including verifying the actual persisted bd labels via bd show.

title: Reopen-task workflow
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Commit creation
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: Extend gitops (or new internal/commit package): CommitTask(ctx, sup, worktreePath, message) asserts the worktree's current branch is not the repository's default branch (defense-in-depth on top of WorktreeManager already only creating task branches, per §15.4 'no direct edits on default branch'), requires a passed diffcheck.Report (re-running the secret scan internally as a hard gate, not advisory), captures base SHA (recorded at worktree creation) and resulting commit SHA after 'git add -A && git commit -m', and records both as evidence (§17.1 'commit' evidence type). MCP tool commit_task.
notes: Per §15.4 and §17.2 (task.yaml/summary.yaml need the commit reference). Security-sensitive (mutates git history) - building myself, tightly coupled to bap.7's diff checker.
close_reason: gitops.WorktreeManager.CommitTask requires a passed diff-check result and a punakawan/-prefixed task branch (defense-in-depth per §15.4's 'no direct edits on default branch') before staging and committing, then records base and resulting commit SHAs. Worktree.BaseSHA is now captured at creation time via a new Inspector.HeadSHA method. Exposed as MCP tool commit_task. 3/3 new gitops tests pass, plus the full MCP lifecycle test exercises a real commit end-to-end.

title: OpenAPI compatibility check
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/openapicheck package wrapping a real Go OpenAPI-diff library (verify current, real API via Context7 before coding - do not guess; oasdiff is the plan's named tool per §13.4/§48). Check(ctx, baseSpecPath, headSpecPath) parses both specs, diffs operations, classifies breaking vs non-breaking changes, and writes api-diff.json into the evidence bundle. MCP tool check_openapi_compatibility.
notes: Per §13.4 ('Use parser libraries plus oasdiff... detect breaking changes, generate compatibility evidence') and §17.1's 'api diff' evidence type. Delegated: self-contained, no repository mutation. Must ground the actual library API in real docs (Context7), not assumed syntax.
close_reason: internal/openapicheck.Check diffs two OpenAPI specs via the real oasdiff library (github.com/oasdiff/oasdiff/diff + checker, API confirmed via Context7 plus direct go doc/source reading for the aggregator checker.CheckBackwardCompatibility, which was not documented by Context7 alone) and classifies breaking vs non-breaking changes, writing api-diff.json evidence (§13.4, §17.2). Exposed as MCP tool check_openapi_compatibility. 5/5 tests pass against real fixture specs (no-change, compatible addition, breaking required-param removal).

title: Finding severity model
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Unit and integration test execution
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/testrun package: Run(ctx, sup, dir, commands []Command) executes each caller-specified command via the existing tools.Supervisor (which already enforces §11.4's working-dir allowlist/env allowlist/timeout/output-size limits), captures exit code/stdout/stderr per command, and writes a tests.json-shaped report into the evidence bundle (§17.2). MCP tool run_tests.
notes: Per §11.3 execution loop steps 'compile'/'run targeted tests' and §17.1 'test report' evidence type. Go does not know project-specific test commands beyond what the caller (Petruk/external role) supplies - this is a thin, policy-bounded executor, not a test framework. Delegated: builds on existing Supervisor, no new mutation surface.
close_reason: internal/testrun.Run executes caller-specified commands via the existing tools.Supervisor (no reimplementation of its safety controls), continuing past a failing command so all results are captured, and writes tests.json evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool run_tests. 4/4 tests pass against real subprocesses.

title: E2E behavior review
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Diff checker
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/diffcheck package: Check(ctx, sup, worktreePath, pol) runs 'git diff --name-only' and 'git diff' via Supervisor, checks each changed file against policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite, scans diff content with a documented heuristic secret-pattern set (AWS key patterns, 'password=', private-key headers, etc - explicitly not a full secret-scanning product), writes diff.patch into the evidence bundle (§17.2), and returns a Report{Allowed, Violations, ChangedFiles}. MCP tool check_diff.
notes: Per §15.4 'block commits containing detected secrets' and 'verify repository root before writing'. Security-sensitive gate that commit_task (bap.10) depends on - building myself.
close_reason: internal/diffcheck.Check stages a worktree's pending changes (git add -A), checks each changed file against policy and a documented heuristic secret-pattern set (AWS keys, PEM headers, password/secret-key assignments), and writes diff.patch evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool check_diff. 4/4 tests pass: clean change allowed, policy-denied path blocked, AWS-key-shaped secret blocked, new+deleted files both captured.

title: Migration review
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Bulk file creation
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: internal/fileops: BulkCreateFiles(pol, worktreeRoot, files []FileSpec) applies the same per-file path-traversal + policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite checks as WriteFile, one file at a time, collecting a per-file FileResult (success or error) rather than requiring all-or-nothing atomicity - the plan does not mandate transactional bulk writes. MCP tool bulk_create_files.
notes: Same risk class as bap.5 (file editing abstraction); built alongside it in the same package. Building myself.
close_reason: internal/fileops.BulkCreateFiles applies WriteFile's checks per file with best-effort (non-transactional) per-file results. Exposed as MCP tool bulk_create_files. Covered by fileops's 5-test suite including TestBulkCreateFilesReportsPerFileOutcome (mixed success/policy-deny/traversal in one call).

title: API compatibility review
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: File editing abstraction
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/fileops package: WriteFile(pol, worktreeRoot, relPath, content) resolves relPath against worktreeRoot with path-traversal rejection (must stay within worktreeRoot after Clean+Abs, per §15.4 'prevent path traversal'), checks policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite(relPath) (reusing existing internal/policy), then writes (creating parent dirs). MCP tool write_file, scoped to a running task's worktree path (derived via a new exported gitops.WorktreePath(workspaceRoot, repoID, taskID) helper so the path formula has one source of truth).
notes: Per §15.4 git/filesystem safety and §3.1's Go-owns-side-effects boundary: the external role proposes edits by calling this tool rather than writing to disk directly, so every mutation is policy-checked and worktree-scoped. Security-sensitive (arbitrary file write) - building myself.
close_reason: internal/fileops.WriteFile checks policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite and rejects path traversal (relative-clean-based, not clamping, so escapes are rejected rather than silently redirected) before writing within a task's worktree. Exposed as MCP tool write_file. 5/5 tests pass covering traversal, absolute-path rejection, and policy-deny enforcement.

title: Test evidence review
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Worktree executor
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/execution package: StartTaskExecution wraps gitops.WorktreeManager.RequestApproval/Approve-check/Create + evidence.NewBundle + evidence.OpenJournal (§11.1 steps 1-4), returning a Session{Worktree, Bundle, Journal}. FinishTaskExecution appends the final journal event and calls WorktreeManager.Remove (step 10). Steps 5-9 (load knowledge/edit/checks/evidence/commit) are separate MCP tools (build_task_context, write_file/bulk_create_files, run_tests, check_diff, commit_task) operating against Session.Worktree.Path - Punakawan never calls an LLM itself (§28), so the external role does the actual edit; Go only owns the orchestration and side effects around it. MCP tools: start_task_execution, finish_task_execution.
notes: Per §11.1's 10-step loop and §11.3. I am building this myself: it is the architecturally central orchestration all other M6 tools plug into, and touches git worktree lifecycle (already security-sensitive per gitops.WorktreeManager's existing approval gate).
close_reason: internal/execution.StartTaskExecution/FinishTaskExecution orchestrate §11.1 steps 1-4 and 10 (worktree creation via gitops.WorktreeManager, evidence bundle/journal open, final status event, worktree removal). Exposed as MCP tools start_task_execution/finish_task_execution, reconstructing session state across the two stateless calls from deterministic paths (gitops.WorktreePath, evidence.OpenJournal). 3/3 unit tests plus a full MCP wire-protocol lifecycle test (TestTaskExecutionLifecycle: start -> write_file -> check_diff -> commit_task -> finish) all pass.

title: Diff review
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Requirement coverage analysis
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Fresh context per task
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New package (or internal/execution/context.go): Build(ctx, ws, sup, store, BuildInput{TaskID, RequirementID, PreviousTaskOutputs, ...}) assembles §11.2's field list (task definition, parent requirement, source excerpts, related decisions, affected symbols/files, required tests, previous outputs, known constraints) by reading the requirement KnowledgeRecord + store.Related + store.ListByType, writes evidence.Bundle.Path('task.yaml') per §17.2, and returns the struct via new MCP tool build_task_context.
notes: Per §11.2: avoid carrying long conversational history across tasks - each task execution gets a fresh, bounded context. Read-only against the knowledge store; mirrors internal/dossier's existing Build pattern. Delegated: low risk, no repo mutation.
close_reason: internal/taskcontext.Build assembles the §11.2 fresh per-task context (parent requirement, source excerpts, related decisions, previous task outputs, known constraints) from the knowledge store plus caller-supplied task fields, and writes it as this task's task.yaml evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool build_task_context. 6/6 tests pass against a real Dolt-backed store.

title: Independent context builder
description: Deliverable of M8: Bagong independent review. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.

title: Ready-task selection
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: internal/beads: add Ready(ctx, sup, dir, opts) wrapping 'bd ready --json' (verify exact flags empirically via bd ready --help before coding, per this repo's established pattern in beads.go). Exposed as MCP tool list_ready_tasks.
notes: Per §9 diagram step 'Petruk executes ready task' and §11.3 execution loop step 1 'claim task'. Delegated: self-contained CLI wrapper, no repository mutation.
close_reason: internal/beads.Ready/ClaimReady wrap 'bd ready --json'/'bd ready --claim --json', with flags/JSON shape verified empirically. Exposed as MCP tools list_ready_tasks/claim_ready_task. 11/11 tests pass against real throwaway bd projects. Found and fixed a real bug during MCP integration: ReadyIssue.Labels/Dependencies lacked omitempty, so an issue with no labels failed the MCP output schema (nil slice marshals to JSON null, not []).

title: Task graph generator
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: Extend internal/tasks with GenerateGraph(ctx, sup, dir, store, items []GraphItem) that batch-creates TaskContracts: pass 1 creates each Beads issue via CreateTaskForRequirement keyed by a caller-supplied LocalKey; pass 2 resolves LocalKey->BeadsID and calls WireDependency for each depends_on edge. Exposed as new MCP tool submit_task_graph (Petruk/Semar submit the decomposition, Go persists deterministically - same pattern as M3 role tools).
notes: Per §10.1-§10.4. Punakawan itself never decomposes a plan (§28 - no LLM calls); the calling role does the reasoning, this tool only creates+wires the resulting graph.
close_reason: internal/tasks.GenerateGraph batch-creates TaskContracts and wires depends_on edges via LocalKey resolution, reusing createTaskForRequirement/WireDependency. Exposed as MCP tool submit_task_graph. 3 new tests (rejects unknown local_key, rejects duplicate local_key, end-to-end with a real wired blocks dependency verified via bd show) plus a full MCP wire-protocol test (TestSubmitTaskGraphAndListReadyTasks) confirming the blocked task is correctly excluded from list_ready_tasks.

title: M8: Bagong independent review
description: Independent context builder, requirement coverage analysis, diff/test/API/migration/E2E review, finding severity model, reopen-task workflow. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: Bagong reviews raw sources and diffs independently; missing acceptance criteria are visible; high-severity findings reopen or create tasks; delivery cannot be marked complete with unresolved blocking findings.

title: M7: Playwright test generation and adjustment
description: Flow-to-Playwright compiler, parameterization, fixture integration, assertion generation, existing-test matching. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: A recorded flow generates a valid Playwright test; existing tests can be updated rather than duplicated; generated tests use semantic locators; test reports and traces become evidence.

title: M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution
description: Task graph generator, ready-task selection, worktree executor, diff checker, test execution, OpenAPI compatibility check, discovered-task handling. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
acceptance_criteria: An approved plan becomes dependency-aware tasks; Petruk executes only ready tasks; each task has isolated changes and evidence; scope expansion becomes a new task; failed checks prevent task completion.
close_reason: All 11 M6 deliverables complete: task graph generation, ready/claim task selection, per-task fresh context, worktree-lifecycle orchestration, file-editing abstraction (single+bulk), diff/secret checking, test execution, OpenAPI compatibility checking, commit creation with git-safety guards, and discovered-task handling, per punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §10-§11. All 13 new MCP tools wired into internal/mcpserver and verified end-to-end over the real MCP wire protocol (submit_task_graph+list_ready_tasks dependency-blocking test, and a full start->write_file->check_diff->commit_task->finish lifecycle test), plus 164/164 repo-wide Go tests passing.

title: M9: Hardening and release
description: Recovery after interruption, adapter sandboxing, secret broker, tool installer/checksums, OpenTelemetry, SBOM, signed release artifacts. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
